title: Velvety Mexican Lamb Stir-fry
description: This high-protein-friendly mexican dish is a velvety umami delight perfect for sick days. It features tender lamb cooked with sous-vide and vibrant, colorful vegetables in a flavorful sauce.

ingredients.name: Sous-vide Lamb
ingredients.notes: Ensure meat is cooked to at least 63°C (145°F) for safety.

instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add chopped bell peppers, red onion, and zucchini to the pan and stir for about 5 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Remove from heat and transfer vegetables to a serving dish. Preheat a wok or skillet over high heat with some additional olive oil.
Place sous-vide lamb in the hot pan and sear on all sides for about 2-3 minutes per side, depending on thickness of meat.
Add garlic powder, soy sauce, tomato paste, bay leaves, salt, and pepper to the wok. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
Pour cooked vegetables back into the wok and continue to stir-fry until mixture thickens slightly, about 3-4 minutes more.
Serve immediately with additional garnishes like fresh cilantro or lime wedges if desired.
